                                <archetype_id>
                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2</value>
                                </archetype_id>
                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                    <items id="text">Medication details</items>
                                    <items id="description">Details about a medication or component of a medication, including strength, form and details of any specific constituents.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                    <items id="text">Combination product</items>
                                    <items id="description">The medication or medication component consists of a number of separate products which are pre-packaged by the manufacturer, for example Canesten Combi.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                    <items id="text">Expiration date</items>
                                    <items id="description">The expiry date and/or time of the medication or medication component, as given by the manufacturer or individual preparing the mixture.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">Any other form of expiry date, such as time from production or depending on storage environment, can be inserted using a specific CLUSTER archetype in the Substance Details slot or added as part of the Description.
 For example: '2017-05-23'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0071">
                                    <items id="text">Form</items>
                                    <items id="description">The formulation or presentation of the medication or medication component.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'tablet', 'capsule', 'cream', 'infusion fluid' or 'inhalation powder'. Coding of the form with a terminology is preferred, where possible. Medicines catalogues may differentiate between administrable form 'solution for injection' and product form 'powder for solution for injection'. The recorded form will depend on the exact context of use but administrable form is likely to be used in most instances.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0080">
                                    <items id="text">Therapeutic</items>
                                    <items id="description">Constituent that alone or in combination with one or more other ingredients is considered to fulfil the intended activity of a medicinal product.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0083">
                                    <items id="text">Adjuvant</items>
                                    <items id="description">Constituent whose primary function is to modify the activity of an active constituent. An adjuvant constituent itself may or may not be therapeutically active.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0084">
                                    <items id="text">Excipient</items>
                                    <items id="description">Constituent that is inert in relation to the intended activity of the medicinal product.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0115">
                                    <items id="text">Strength (concentration)</items>
                                    <items id="description">The strength of the medication or medication component, as a concentration.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">This element is used for liquid or semisolid medications, or medications intended to be diluted in a liquid before administration. For example: '10 mg/ml', '20 mg/g', '5 %', '10,000 SQ-U/ml'. If the 'Strength (presentation)' cluster is used, this element becomes redundant.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0127">
                                    <items id="text">Role</items>
                                    <items id="description">The role of the medication or medication component within a mixture.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0132">
                                    <items id="text">Name</items>
                                    <items id="description">The name of the medication or medication component.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Zinacef 750 mg powder' or 'cefuroxim'. This item should be coded if possible, using for example, RxNorm, DM+D, Australian Medicines Terminology or FEST. Usage of this element will vary according to context of use. This element may be omitted where the name of the medication is recorded in the parent INSTRUCTION or ACTION archetype, and this archetype is only used to record that the form must be or was 'liquid'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0133">
                                    <items id="text">Note</items>
                                    <items id="description">Narrative description of the medication or medication component where it is not possible to describe this fully using structured elements.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0138">
                                    <items id="text">Constituent</items>
                                    <items id="description">Details of an ingredient or product used to make up a mixed pack, preparation or infusion.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">This slot is intended to be used to add details about constituents of the medication or medication component, using nested instances of this archetype. This is not normally required other than where a mixture is being described.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0139">
                                    <items id="text">Amount</items>
                                    <items id="description">The amount of medication or medication component.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: '1 ml', '1.5 mg', '1000 ml'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0141">
                                    <items id="text">Structured details</items>
                                    <items id="description">Additional details about the medication or medication component.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: detailed information about the drug class or intended routes, or additional expiry information.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0142">
                                    <items id="text">Category</items>
                                    <items id="description">The category of the medication or medication component, with regard to manufacturing or preparation, and the number of ingredients.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Paracetamol/codeine' is a Multi-ingredient product, while 'Morphine 60 mg + Haloperidol 2 mg + Midazolam 5 mg' is an Ad-hoc mixture, whose composition is fully specified within the order.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0143">
                                    <items id="text">Ad-hoc mixture</items>
                                    <items id="description">The medication or medication component is composed of a mixture of ingredients specified within the order. These are typically prepared by pharmacy or ward personnel to suit individual patients.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0144">
                                    <items id="text">Multi-ingredient product</items>
                                    <items id="description">The medication or medication component consists of a number of active ingredients which are pre-combined into a single form such as a tablet, cream or powder by the manufacturer, for example Paracetamol/codeine.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0145">
                                    <items id="text">Single-ingredient product</items>
                                    <items id="description">The medication or medication component is a manufactured product containing a single active ingredient.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0146">
                                    <items id="text">Ingredient</items>
                                    <items id="description">The medication or medication component is an individual ingredient of the medication. This term is used when the archetype is nested within a parent instance of itself, to describe the individual ingredients of a medication.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0148">
                                    <items id="text">Alternate amount</items>
                                    <items id="description">An equivalent representation of the amount of the medication or medication component.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: for a medication with a strength of '5 mg/ml' and where the Amount is '1 ml', and the value recorded in this data element would be '5 mg'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0150">
                                    <items id="text">Lot number</items>
                                    <items id="description">The identifier assigned to the production batch by the manufacturer during production.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0151">
                                    <items id="text">Manufacturer</items>
                                    <items id="description">The manufacturer of the medication or medication component.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Abbott'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0152">
                                    <items id="text">Strength (presentation)</items>
                                    <items id="description">The strength of the medication or medication component, expressed as a ratio.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">In some cases, as for liquid or semisolid medications, the denominator of the strength ratio is a physical quantity, for example 2 mg/5 ml. In some of these cases the denominator also reflects the actual volume of the component: 5 ml in the previous example. In this case the 'Strength (concentration)' would be 0.4 mg/ml. In other cases, where the strength involves a denominator which is not a physical quantity, for example 4 mg/tablet, the denominator is expressed as a unitary value '1' with a unit of 'tablet'. This arrangement was chosen to align with the approach adopted by the ISO IDMP standard for medication catalogues. If the 'Strength (concentration)' element is used, this cluster becomes redundant.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0153">
                                    <items id="text">Strength numerator</items>
                                    <items id="description">The numerator of the strength fraction.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: For a presentation strength of '300 µg/0.3 ml', the strength numerator is '300 µg'. For a presentation strength of '100 mg/tablet', the strength numerator value is '100 mg'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0157">
                                    <items id="text">Strength denominator</items>
                                    <items id="description">The denominator of the strength fraction.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: For a presentation strength of '300 µg/0.3 ml', the strength denominator value is '0.3 ml'. For a presentation strength of '100 mg/tablet', the strength denominator value is '1 tablet'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                            </children>

                                <archetype_id>
                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.dosage.v2</value>
                                </archetype_id>
                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                    <items id="text">Dosage</items>
                                    <items id="description">The combination of a medication amount and administration timing for a single day, in the context of a medication order or medication management.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: '2 tablets at 6pm' or '20 mg three times per day'. Please note: this cluster allows multiple occurrences to enable representation of a complete set of dose patterns for a single dose direction.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0037">
                                    <items id="text">Daily timing</items>
                                    <items id="description">Structured details about the timing pattern for a single day.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'in the morning', 'at 0600, 1400, 2100'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0102">
                                    <items id="text">Administration duration</items>
                                    <items id="description">The period of time over which a single dose of the medication or vaccine should be administered.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Administer over 10 minutes'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0134">
                                    <items id="text">Administration rate</items>
                                    <items id="description">The rate at which the medication, such as an infusion, is to be administered.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: '200 ml/h'. Use the text data type to record non- or semi-quantifiable instructions.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0135">
                                    <items id="text">Dose formula</items>
                                    <items id="description">The formula used to calculate the dose amount or administration rate where this is dependent on some other factor, such as body weight or surface area.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: '10 mg/kg/day'. The result of this formula would normally be held in Dose or Administration rate/duration. Where clinical measurements such as body weight is used in the dose calculation, a LINK attribute should used to specify which particular measurement has been used.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0144">
                                    <items id="text">Dose Quantity</items>
                                    <items id="description">The amount of medication administered at one time.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 1 mg, 1.5 ml, 0.125 g; or 1-2 ml, 12.5-20.5 mg.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0164">
                                    <items id="text">Dosage sequence</items>
                                    <items id="description">The intended position of this dosage within the overall sequence of dosages.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: '1', '2', '3'. 
Where multiple dosages are expressed, the 'Pattern sequence' makes the order in which they should be executed explicit. For example: (1) 1 tab in the morning, (2) 2 tab at 2pm, (3) 1 tab at night.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0176">
                                    <items id="text">Alternate dose</items>
                                    <items id="description">An alternate representation of the amount of medication administered at one time.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example, can be used to represent a unit-dose based value such as 'tabs', when the Dose amount is expressed as an SI unit such as 'mg', or where it is required to express the total volume of an infusion as well as the amount of the active ingredient.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0178">
                                    <items id="text">Dose description</items>
                                    <items id="description">Text description of the dose.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: "Apply ointment to affected area until it glistens". This element is intended to allow implementers to use the structures for increasing/tapering dosages without necessarily specifying the doses in a structured way.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                            </children>

                                <archetype_id>
                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.adverse_reaction_event.v1</value>
                                </archetype_id>
                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                    <items id="text">Adverse reaction event</items>
                                    <items id="description">Information about a specific adverse reaction event caused by exposure to a specific substance.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                    <items id="text">Specific substance</items>
                                    <items id="description">The substance considered to be responsible for the specific adverse reaction event.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Amoxycillin'. Only an individual substance is a valid entry in 'Specific substance'. A substance may be a compound of simpler substances, for example a medicinal product. If the 'Substance' recorded in the archetype in which this archetype is nested is an individual substance and not a substance class, then it may be duplicated in this data element, or this element may be redundant. It is strongly recommended that 'Specific substance' be coded with a terminology, for example: RxNorm, SNOMED CT, DM+D, NDFRT, ICD-9, ICD-10, UNI, ATC or CPT.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                    <items id="text">Certainty</items>
                                    <items id="description">The degree of clinical certainty that the 'Manifestation' in this reaction event was caused by exposure to the identified 'Specific substance'.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">The free text data type will allow for local variation by enabling other value sets to be applied to this data element in a template - in this situation it is recommended that values should be coded using a terminology.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                    <items id="text">Confirmed</items>
                                    <items id="description">It has been objectively verified that the 'Manifestation' in this reaction event was caused by exposure to the identified 'Substance'. This may include clinical evidence by testing, re-challenge or observation.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                    <items id="text">Manifestation</items>
                                    <items id="description">Clinical symptoms and/or signs that are observed or associated with the adverse reaction.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">Manifestation can be expressed as a single word, phrase or brief description. For example: nausea, rash. 'No reaction' may be appropriate where a previous reaction has been noted but the reaction did not re-occur after further exposure. It is preferable that 'Manifestation' should be coded with a terminology, where possible. The values entered here may be used to display on an application screen as part of a list of adverse reactions.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                    <items id="text">Reaction description</items>
                                    <items id="description">Narrative description about the adverse reaction as a whole, including details of the manifestation if required.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0008">
                                    <items id="text">Onset of reaction</items>
                                    <items id="description">The date and/or time of the onset of the reaction.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0009">
                                    <items id="text">Duration of reaction</items>
                                    <items id="description">The total amount of time that the manifestation of the adverse reaction persisted.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0010">
                                    <items id="text">Severity of reaction</items>
                                    <items id="description">Clinical assessment of the severity of the reaction event as a whole, potentially considering multiple different manifestations.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">It is acknowledged that this assessment is very subjective. There may be some some specific practice domains where objective scales have been applied. Objective scales can be included in this model using the 'Reaction details' Cluster.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0011">
                                    <items id="text">Mild</items>
                                    <items id="description">Causes mild physiological effects.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0012">
                                    <items id="text">Moderate</items>
                                    <items id="description">Causes moderate physiological effects.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0013">
                                    <items id="text">Severe</items>
                                    <items id="description">Causes severe physiological effects.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0014">
                                    <items id="text">Reaction details</items>
                                    <items id="description">Additional details about the adverse reaction.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">May include structured detail about symptoms; the anatomical location of the manifestation; grading, classification or formal severity assessments such as Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events; or a media representation.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0015">
                                    <items id="text">Initial exposure</items>
                                    <items id="description">The date and/or time of the first exposure to the Substance for this Reaction Event.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">Exposure can be more complicated by more than one exposure events leading to a reaction. Further details about the nature of the exposure can be provided by use of additional archetypes in the 'Exposure details' SLOT or as text in the 'Exposure description'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0016">
                                    <items id="text">Duration of exposure</items>
                                    <items id="description">The total amount of time the individual was exposed to the identified 'Specific substance'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0017">
                                    <items id="text">Route of exposure</items>
                                    <items id="description">The route by which the subject was exposed to the identified 'Specific substance'.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">Coding of the Route of Exposure with a terminology should be used wherever possible.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0018">
                                    <items id="text">Exposure description</items>
                                    <items id="description">Narrative description about the exposure to the identified 'Specific substance'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0019">
                                    <items id="text">Exposure details</items>
                                    <items id="description">Additional details about exposure to the 'Specific substance'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0020">
                                    <items id="text">Clinical management description</items>
                                    <items id="description">Narrative description about the clinical management provided.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0021">
                                    <items id="text">Clinical management details</items>
                                    <items id="description">Additional structured details about clinical management for this reaction event.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0022">
                                    <items id="text">Reporting details</items>
                                    <items id="description">Additional structured details required for reporting to regulatory bodies.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0023">
                                    <items id="text">Information source</items>
                                    <items id="description">Details about the provenance of the information.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">This SLOT is intended to provide details about the source of information for this particular 'Reaction event'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0024">

                                    <items id="description">Additional narrative about the adverse reaction event not captured in other fields.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0025">
                                    <items id="text">Supporting clinical record information</items>
                                    <items id="description">Link to further information about the presentation and findings that exist elsewhere in the health record, including allergy test reports.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">For example, presenting symptoms, examination findings, diagnosis etc.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0026">
                                    <items id="text">Unconfirmed</items>
                                    <items id="description">It has not been objectively verified that the 'Manifestation' in this reaction event was caused by exposure to the identified 'Substance'.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                    <items id="text">Refuted</items>
                                    <items id="description">It has been disputed or disproven that the 'Manifestation' in this reaction event was caused by exposure to the identified 'Substance', with a sufficient level of clinical certainty to justify invalidating the assertion.                                 <archetype_id>
                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_source_information.v0</value>
                                </archetype_id>
                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                    <items id="text">CH VACD source information</items>
                                    <items id="description">Source, provenance, verification and data-quality information associated with CH VACD immunization data.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                    <items id="text">Verification status</items>
                                    <items id="description">The verification status of the CH VACD immunization source information, indicating whether the immunization entry has been verified. This field is mandatory in the CH VACD Immunization profile.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.extension:verificationStatus.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the CH VACD verification status value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/ValueSet/ch-vacd-verification-status-vs&_format=json</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                    <items id="text">Source immunization identifier</items>
                                    <items id="description">Identifier assigned to the source CH VACD immunization entry for traceability, matching or reconciliation.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">This data element represents the business identifier of the source Immunization resource. It should be distinguished from the FHIR logical resource id, which is mapped separately from Immunization.id if required. The identifier may be used for traceability, matching, deduplication or reconciliation of imported CH VACD immunization entries.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.identifier.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                    <items id="text">Source data details</items>
                                    <items id="description"></items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                    <items id="text">Occurrence data absent reason</items>
                                    <items id="description">Reason why the structured vaccination administration date or time is absent from the source data.

This data element should be constrained to DV_CODED_TEXT in a template and bound to the HL7 FHIR Data Absent Reason value set, where possible. It describes why the structured administration date or time is missing and must not be used as the vaccination administration time.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.occurrenceDateTime.extension:data-absent-reason.valueCode.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the HL7 FHIR Data Absent Reason value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/data-absent-reason&_format=json</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                    <items id="text">Source recorded date</items>
                                    <items id="description">Date and time when the source CH VACD immunization entry was first recorded in the source system.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">This is the recording time of the source immunization entry, not the vaccination administration time and not the openEHR commit time.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.recorded.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                            </children>

                                <archetype_id>
                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_immunization_protocol.v0</value>
                                </archetype_id>
                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                    <items id="text">CH VACD immunization protocol</items>
                                    <items id="description">CH VACD-specific protocol details for an administered vaccination.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                    <items id="text">Target disease</items>
                                    <items id="description">The vaccine-preventable disease or condition targeted by the vaccination protocol, according to the CH VACD target disease value set.

This data element should be constrained to DV_CODED_TEXT in a template and bound to the CH VACD target disease value set, where possible.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.protocolApplied.targetDisease.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the CH VACD target diseases and illnesses undergone for immunization value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/ValueSet/ch-vacd-targetdiseasesandillnessesundergoneforimmunization-vs&_format=json.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                    <items id="text">Dose potency</items>
                                    <items id="description">Details about whether the administered vaccine dose is considered to have reduced potency or effectiveness.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                    <items id="text">Subpotent dose</items>
                                    <items id="description">Indicates whether the administered vaccine dose is considered to be subpotent.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.isSubpotent.</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                                <term_definitions code="at0008">
                                    <items id="text">Reason for subpotency</items>
                                    <items id="description">The reason why the administered vaccine dose is considered to be subpotent.

This data element should be coded with a terminology in a template, where possible.

FHIR mapping: Immunization.subpotentReason.</items>
                                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the HL7 FHIR immunization subpotent reason value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/immunization-subpotent-reason&_format=json</items>
                                </term_definitions>
                            </children>
                            <cardinality>
                                <is_ordered>false</is_ordered>
                                <is_unique>false</is_unique>
                                <interval>
                                    <lower_included>true</lower_included>
                                    <upper_included>false</upper_included>

                                    <upper_unbounded>true</upper_unbounded>
                                    <lower>1</lower>
                                </interval>
                            </cardinality>
                        </attributes>
                    </children>
                </attributes>
                <archetype_id>
                    <value>openEHR-EHR-ACTION.medication.v1</value>
                </archetype_id>
                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                    <items id="text">Medication management</items>
                    <items id="description">Any activity related to the planning, scheduling, prescription management, dispensing, administration, cessation and other use of a medication, vaccine, nutritional product or other therapeutic item.</items>
                    <items id="comment">This is not limited to activities performed based on medication orders from clinicians, but could also include for example taking over the counter medication.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0002">
                    <items id="text">Prescription issued</items>
                    <items id="description">A prescription has been issued for the medication.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0003">
                    <items id="text">Prescription dispensed</items>
                    <items id="description">The ordered medication has been dispensed, for example from a pharmacy to the patient.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0004">
                    <items id="text">Medication course commenced</items>
                    <items id="description">The medication has been taken by, or administered to, the patient for the first time. Although in some settings this significant date may be computable as the first of several administrations, in other settings, such as primary care, specific administration dates are not readily available.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0005">
                    <items id="text">Medication reassessed</items>
                    <items id="description">The individual medication has been reassessed, for example whether the medication should still be taken. This is not intended to capture review of the medication list.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0006">
                    <items id="text">Dose administered</items>
                    <items id="description">A single administration of the medication has taken place.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0007">
                    <items id="text">Medication course completed</items>
                    <items id="description">The medication course has been completed as planned.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0008">
                    <items id="text">Prescription supply delayed</items>
                    <items id="description">The prescription has not been dispensed due to a technical or pharamaceutical supply issue.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0009">
                    <items id="text">Administrations suspended</items>
                    <items id="description">The administration of the medication has been suspended until further notice. No further doses should be given until the restart date or conditions have been met. When setting the date/conditions for restart after suspending, a step from ‘Administrations suspended’ and back to 'Medication start date/condition set' should be performed.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0010">
                    <items id="text">Prescription re-issued</items>
                    <items id="description">A prescription has been re-issued for an existing medication order.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0011">
                    <items id="text">Prescription re-authorisation pending</items>
                    <items id="description">Issue of the prescription is awaiting re-authorisation by a clinician.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0012">
                    <items id="text">Medication course cancelled</items>
                    <items id="description">The planned course of medication has been cancelled prior to any administration.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0013">
                    <items id="text">Medication course postponed</items>
                    <items id="description">The scheduled medication course has been postponed prior to any administration.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0015">
                    <items id="text">Medication course stopped</items>
                    <items id="description">Administration of the medication has been ceased during the period of the intended course.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0016">
                    <items id="text">Medication start date/condition set</items>
                    <items id="description">The time to start the medication, or other starting condition, has been set.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0017">
                    <items id="text">Tree</items>
                    <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0018">
                    <items id="text">Dose administration omitted</items>
                    <items id="description">An administration of the medication has been withheld and not given. There is no expectation that it will be given later, though the next dose (if any) should be administered according to the original order.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0020">
                    <items id="text">Vaccine administered</items>
                    <items id="description">Name of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic/prescribable item which was the focus of the activity.</items>
                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the CH VACD Swissmedic vaccine value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/ValueSet/ch-vacd-swissmedic-vaccines-vs&_format=json</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0021">
                    <items id="text">Reason</items>
                    <items id="description">Reason that the pathway step for the identified medication was carried out.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Postponed - Patient not avalable at administration time', 'Cancelled - Adverse reaction'. Note: This is not the reason for the medication instruction, but rather the specific reason that a care step was carried out, and will often be used to document some variance from the original order.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0024">
                    <items id="text">Note</items>
                    <items id="description">Additional narrative about the activity or pathway step not captured in other fields, including details of any variance between the intended action and the action actually performed.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Patient was in radiology department', 'Accidental injection into blood vessel during IM administration'.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0025">
                    <items id="text">Dose number</items>
                    <items id="description">The sequence number specific to the pathway step being recorded.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: Vaccine sequence number.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0030">
                    <items id="text">Tree</items>
                    <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0033">
                    <items id="text">Patient guidance</items>
                    <items id="description">Any guidance, instructions or advice given to the subject of care or personal carer at the time of the pathway step.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: 'Avoid grapefruit' , 'Take at least 2 hours before bedtime', 'Take with food'. May be coded with a terminology when clinically appropriate.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0039">
                    <items id="text">Major change to order</items>
                    <items id="description">A major change to the order was required, resulting in this order being stopped and a replacement order being started.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0041">
                    <items id="text">Minor change to order</items>
                    <items id="description">The medication order has been changed in a manner which does not require a new instruction/order to be issued, according to local clinical rules.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0043">
                    <items id="text">Original scheduled date/time</items>
                    <items id="description">The datetime at which the medication action was scheduled to occur.</items>
                    <items id="comment">Used to compare variance from actual action time where this is not readily calculable from the original instruction.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0044">
                    <items id="text">Dose administration deferred</items>
                    <items id="description">An administration of a dose of the medication has been delayed but is expected to be given as soon as possible.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0053">
                    <items id="text">Additional details</items>
                    <items id="description">Further structured details of the action, possibly specific to a pathway step.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0085">
                    <items id="text">Extension</items>
                    <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0103">
                    <items id="text">Vaccination protocol</items>
                    <items id="description">Unique identifier for the medication order.</items>
                    <items id="comment">Comment: This data element allows for multiple occurrences to be defined more explicitly at run-time, if required.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0104">
                    <items id="text">Medication details</items>
                    <items id="description">Structured details about the medication including strength, form and constituent substances.</items>
                    <items id="comment">Use this SLOT where the detailed description of the dispensed, authorised or administered item needs to be explicitly stated. For example: the form, strength, any diluents or mixture of ingredients.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0106">
                    <items id="text">Prescription re-authorised</items>
                    <items id="description">The original medication order has been re-authorised to allow repeat prescription or dispensing. In some jurisdictions an entirely new order must be issued in these circumstances.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0109">
                    <items id="text">Medication recommended</items>
                    <items id="description">The medication has been recommended but no steps have been taken to initiate prescribing.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0131">
                    <items id="text">Amount</items>
                    <items id="description">Specific details about the amount of the medication item.</items>
                    <items id="comment">CLUSTER.dosage is intended to carry details of medication order changes or dose administrations, whilst CLUSTER.medication_supply_amount is intended for details of dispensing-related activity.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0132">
                    <items id="text">Substitution</items>
                    <items id="description">Subsitution action taken by the person administering or dispensing the drug.</items>
                    <items id="comment">In many jurisdictions, substitution of an ordered item as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases substitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0133">
                    <items id="text">Substitution reason</items>
                    <items id="description">The reason or justification for the substitution action taken.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: "Generic alternative contains a substance that is not tolerated by the patient." May be coded with a terminology when clinically appropriate</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0138">
                    <items id="text">Substitution performed</items>
                    <items id="description">A medication was substituted which is bioequivalent to that ordered.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0139">
                    <items id="text">Substitution not performed</items>
                    <items id="description">Although allowed by the medication order a bioequvalent medication was not substituted.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0140">
                    <items id="text">Administration details</items>
                    <items id="description">Details of body site and administration of the medication.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0141">
                    <items id="text">Body site</items>
                    <items id="description">Structured description of the site of administration of the ordered item.</items>
                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the HL7 FHIR immunization site value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/immunization-site&_format=json</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0142">
                    <items id="text">Structured body site</items>
                    <items id="description">Structured description of the site of administration of the medication, vaccine or therapeutic good.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: "On the medial skin surface of the left forearm, from 10 cm to 20 cm distally from the point of the elbow".</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0143">
                    <items id="text">Administration method</items>
                    <items id="description">The technique or device by which the ordered item was, or is to be, administered.</items>
                    <items id="comment">Comment: For example: ' via Z-track injection'; 'via nebuliser'. Coding of the method with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0144">
                    <items id="text">Administration device</items>
                    <items id="description">Details of the medical device used to assist administration of the medication.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0145">
                    <items id="text">Prescription awaiting authorisation</items>
                    <items id="description">Draft prescription has been prepared and is awaiting confirmation from an authorised clinician. May be used where reauthorisations are performed as a batch. This careflow_step may have a status of 'planned' or 'active', reflecting the need to to handle new orders as well as re-authorised orders.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0147">
                    <items id="text">Route</items>
                    <items id="description">The route by which the ordered item was, or is to be, administered into the subject's body.</items>
                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the CH VACD route of administration value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/ValueSet/ch-vacd-route-of-administration-vs&_format=json</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0148">
                    <items id="text">Medication prepared</items>
                    <items id="description">The medication has been physically prepared.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: preparation of an intravenous mixture.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0149">
                    <items id="text">Double-checked?</items>
                    <items id="description">The pathway step has been checked by a separate individual.</items>
                    <items id="comment">Details of the individual performing the double-check can be carried in the Reference Model element "Participation".</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0150">
                    <items id="text">Prescription cancelled</items>
                    <items id="description">The prescription was cancelled prior to being issued.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0151">
                    <items id="text">Prescription invalid or expired</items>
                    <items id="description">Prescription has been invalidated or has expired without being fulfilled.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0152">
                    <items id="text">Prescription fulfilled</items>
                    <items id="description">The prescription has been fulfilled successfully.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0153">
                    <items id="text">Medication authorised</items>
                    <items id="description">The medication has been formally authorised for use.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0154">
                    <items id="text">Restart date/time</items>
                    <items id="description">The date/time on which the medication course is set to restart, as per the "Administrations suspended" pathway step.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: 2017-10-29</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0155">
                    <items id="text">Restart criterion</items>
                    <items id="description">The criterion which triggers the medication course to restart, as per the "Administrations suspended" pathway step.</items>
                    <items id="comment">For example: "On day 2 after surgery".</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0156">
                    <items id="text">Reason code</items>
                    <items id="description">The clinical reason for the medication activity.</items>
                    <items id="comment">FHIR terminology binding to the HL7 FHIR immunization reason value set. Expansion via tx.fhir.ch:
https://tx.fhir.ch/r4/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/immunization-reason&_format=json</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0157">
                    <items id="text">Medication double checked</items>
                    <items id="description">The prepared medication has been double checked.</items>
                </term_definitions>
                <term_definitions code="at0158">
                    <items id="text">Dose administration aborted</items>
                    <items id="description">An administration of a dose of the medication was started but was subsequently aborted.</items>
                </term_definitions>
            </children>
            <cardinality>
                <is_ordered>false</is_ordered>
                <is_unique>false</is_unique>
                <interval>
                    <lower_included>true</lower_included>
                    <upper_included>false</upper_included>
                    <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                    <upper_unbounded>true</upper_unbounded>
                    <lower>1</lower>
                </interval>
            </cardinality>
        </attributes>
        <archetype_id>
            <value>openEHR-EHR-COMPOSITION.vaccination_list.v0</value>
        </archetype_id>
        <template_id>
            <value>ch-vacd-vaccination-record.v1-alpha</value>
        </template_id>
        <term_definitions code="at0000">
            <items id="text">CH VACD Vaccination record</items>
            <items id="description">A persistent and managed list of vaccinations that have been administered to an individual over time.</items>
        </term_definitions>
        <term_definitions code="at0004">
            <items id="text">Tree</items>
            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
        </term_definitions>
        <term_definitions code="at0005">
            <items id="text">Extension</items>
            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
            <items id="comment">For example: Local hospital departmental infomation or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
        </term_definitions>
    </definition>
    <constraints>
        <attributes>
            <rm_attribute_name>value</rm_attribute_name>
            <children>
                <default_value xsi:type="DV_IDENTIFIER" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>
            </children>
            <differential_path>[openEHR-EHR-COMPOSITION.vaccination_list.v0]/content[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.medication.v1]/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2]/items[at0150, 'Lot number']</differential_path>
        </attributes>
        <attributes>
            <rm_attribute_name>value</rm_attribute_name>
            <children>
                <default_value xsi:type="DV_IDENTIFIER" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>
            </children>
            <differential_path>[openEHR-EHR-COMPOSITION.vaccination_list.v0]/content[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.medication.v1]/protocol[at0030]/items[at0103, 'Vaccination protocol']</differential_path>
        </attributes>
    </constraints>
    <view>
        <constraints path="[openEHR-EHR-COMPOSITION.vaccination_list.v0]/content[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.medication.v1]/description">
            <items id="VisibleInView">
                <value xsi:type="xs:string" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">description</value>
            </items>
        </constraints>
    </view>
</template>

  - name: "feederAudit"
    with:

    mappingCode: "feederAudit(fhir)"

grammar: FHIRConnect/v0.9.0
type: model
metadata:
  name:  ACTION.medication.v1
  version: 0.0.1-alpha 
spec: 
  system: FHIR
  version: R4
  openEhrConfig:
    archetype: openEHR-EHR-ACTION.medication.v1
    revision: 1.3.0
  fhirConfig:
    structureDefinition: http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Immunization

mappings:
  - name: meta
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.meta"
      openehr: "$composition"
    manual:
      - name: "profile"
        fhir:
          - path: "profile"
            value: "http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/StructureDefinition/ch-vacd-immunization"


  - name: "time"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.occurrence.as(DateTime)"
      openehr: "$archetype/time"
    followedBy:
      mappings:
        - name: "sourceDataDetails"
          with:
            fhir: "extension"
            openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_source_information.v0]/items[at0003]"
          fhirCondition:
            targetRoot: "extension"
            targetAttribute: "url"
            operator: "one of"
            criteria: "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/data-absent-reason"
          followedBy:
            mappings:
              - name: "sourceDataDetailsDataAbsentReason"
                with:
                  fhir: "value"
                  openehr: "items[at0004]"
              - name: "url"
                with:
                  fhir: "$fhirRoot"
                manual:
                  - name: "url"
                    fhir:
                      - path: "url"
                        value: "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/data-absent-reason"

  - name: "ismTransition"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource"
      openehr: "$archetype/ism_transition/careflow_step"
    manual:
      - name: "completed"
        fhir:
          - path: "status"
            value: "completed"
        openehr:
          - path: "defining_code/terminology_id"
            value: "local"
          - path: "defining_code/code_string"
            value: "at0006"
          - path: "value"
            value: "Dose administration"

  - name: "otherParticipations"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.performer"
      openehr: "$archetype/other_participations"
    followedBy:
      mappings:
        - name: "performerPractitionerRole"
          with:
            fhir: "actor"
            openehr: "performer"
        - name: "function"
          with:
            fhir: "function"
            openehr: "function"

  - name: "vaccineCode"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.vaccineCode"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[at0020]"

  - name: "reasonCode"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.reasonCode"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[at0156]"

  - name: "route"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.route"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[at0140]/items[at0147]"

  - name: "bodySite"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.site"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[at0140]/items[at0141]"

  - name: "doseNumber"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.protocolApplied.doseNumber"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[at0025]"

  - name: "comment"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.note.text"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[at0024]"

  - name: "medicationDetails"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.extension"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2]"
    fhirCondition:
      targetRoot: "$resource.extension"
      targetAttribute: "url"
      operator: "one of"
      criteria: "http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/StructureDefinition/ch-vacd-ext-immunization-medication-reference"
    followedBy:
      mappings:
        - name: "url"
          with:
            fhir: "$fhirRoot"
          manual:
            - name: "url"
              fhir:
                - path: "url"
                  value: "http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/StructureDefinition/ch-vacd-ext-immunization-medication-reference"

        - name: "medicationReference"
          with:
            fhir: "value"
            openehr: "$reference"
          reference:
            resourceType: "Medication"
            mappings:
              - name: "medicationSlot"
                with:
                  fhir: "$fhirRoot"
                  openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2]"
                slotArchetype: "CLUSTER.medication.v2"

  - name: "immunizationManufacturer"
    with:

      fhir: "$resource.manufacturer.as(Reference).display"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2]/items[at0151]"


  - name: "immunizationLotNumber"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.lotNumber"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2]/items[at0150]"

  - name: "immunizationExpirationDate"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.expirationDate"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication.v2]/items[at0003]"

  - name: "doseQuantity"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.doseQuantity"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.dosage.v2]/items[at0144, 'Dose Quantity']"

  - name: "adverseReaction"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.reaction"
      openehr: "$archetype/description[at0017]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.adverse_reaction_event.v1]"
    followedBy:
      mappings:
        - name: "display"
          with:
            fhir: "detail.as(Reference).display"
            openehr: "items[at0007]"
        - name: "date"
          with:
            fhir: "date"
            openehr: "items[at0008]"

  - name: "protocol"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.protocolApplied"
      openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_immunization_protocol.v0]"
    followedBy:
      mappings:
        - name: "targetDisease"
          with:
            fhir: "targetDisease"
            openehr: "items[at0003]"

  - name: "subpotentDose"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.isSubpotent"
      openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_immunization_protocol.v0]/items[at0006]/items[at0007]"

  - name: "subpotentReason"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.subpotentReason"
      openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_immunization_protocol.v0]/items[at0006]/items[at0008]"

  - name: "sourceInformation"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.identifier"
      openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_source_information.v0]/items[at0002]"

  - name: "verificationStatus"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource.extension"
      openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_source_information.v0]"
    fhirCondition:
      targetRoot: "$resource.extension"
      targetAttribute: "url"
      operator: "one of"
      criteria: "http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/StructureDefinition/ch-vacd-ext-verification-status"
    followedBy:
      mappings:
        - name: "value"
          with:
            fhir: "value"
            openehr: "items[at0001]"
        - name: "url"
          with:
            fhir: "$fhirRoot"
          manual:
            - name: "url"
              fhir:
                - path: "url"
                  value: "http://fhir.ch/ig/ch-vacd/StructureDefinition/ch-vacd-ext-verification-status"

  - name: "sourceDataDetailsRecorded"
    with:
      fhir: "recorded"
      openehr: "$archetype/protocol[at0030]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.ch_vacd_source_information.v0]/items[at0003]/items[at0005]"

  - name: "feederAudit"
    with:
      fhir: "$resource"
      openehr: "$archetype/feeder_audit"
    mappingCode: "feederAudit(fhir)"

    mappingCode: "feederAudit(fhir)"

        <other_details id="PARENT:MD5-CAM-1.0.1">3B0765A6B395C9D01595FDF2D440E6CB</other_details>
        <details>
            <language>
                <terminology_id>
                    <value>ISO_639-1</value>
                </terminology_id>
                <code_string>en</code_string>
            </language>
            <purpose>An openEHR template for recording one administered vaccine dose based on the Swiss CH VACD Immunization profile.</purpose>
            <use>Use to record one administered immunization event, including the administered vaccine, administration date and time, route, anatomical administration site, dose sequence, administered quantity, product and batch details, target disease, and CH VACD-specific source and verification information.

This template is intended for representing vaccination administration data imported from, exchanged with, or aligned to the Swiss CH VACD Immunization Administration Document. It may be used when transforming FHIR CH VACD Immunization resources into openEHR, where each Immunization resource represents one administered vaccine dose.

Use the ACTION.medication entry to represent the administered vaccine event. Use medication and dosage clusters for product, batch and dose details. Use CH VACD-specific clusters for immunization protocol and source information where required by the implementation.

The original source data should be retained using feeder_audit or an agreed source-content mechanism to support traceability and validation.</use>
            <misuse>Not to be used to record a planned, recommended or prescribed vaccination that has not yet been administered. Use an appropriate instruction, plan or recommendation model for planned immunizations.

Not to be used to record non-administration, refusal, contraindication or cancellation as if a vaccine had been administered. FHIR Immunization resources with status not-done or entered-in-error require separate handling.

Not to be used as a complete vaccination record or lifelong vaccination list by itself. A vaccination record or immunization summary should be represented using an appropriate persistent composition or summary/list model that can aggregate multiple administered immunizations.

Not to be used as a full replacement for the CH VACD FHIR document or for preserving source provenance. The original FHIR source content and document-level metadata should be retained separately where traceability, legal auditability or re-export is required.

Not to be used to record persistent allergy, intolerance, contraindication, risk factor or past vaccine-preventable disease information. Use appropriate adverse reaction, condition, problem, risk factor or allergy/intolerance models for these data.</misuse>
        </details>
    </description>

                                                <precedence_overridden>false</precedence_overridden>
                                                <left_operand xsi:type="EXPR_LEAF">
